lineage.summary: The pinned CME XML is a repository version of U-M's 2006 APG1531 encoding, which encodes selected pages from Friedrich W. D. Brie's 1906/1908 two-volume edition. Brie combines the common prose Brut through 1333 with separately transmitted continuations, extracts, appendices, and poems. The selected content ends in 1475; the U-M header's 1479 claim is retained only as an attributed statement about a broader continuation tradition. Seventeen base-text relations, fifteen collations, complete and selected digital surrogates, and access-specific rights are kept as distinct layers.

catalogSummary.editor: Friedrich W. D. Brie; Frederick J. Furnivall
genres: Verse chronicle and occasional political verse (components)
catalogSummary.title: The Brut, or The Chronicles of England

catalogingSubject.label: The Brut, or The Chronicles of England (Brie/APG1531 selected compilation)
catalogingSubject.scope_note: The selected medieval contents printed by Friedrich W. D. Brie in EETS Original Series 131 and 136: the earlier anonymous English prose translation of the French Brut through 1333, followed by a deliberately selected set of independently transmitted English continuations, extracts, appendices, and poems whose selected section K endpoint is 1475. This is not one homogeneous medieval composition and not the entire contents of any one manuscript. Later integration should preserve the edition/selection boundary unless component works are separately researched and identified.

summaries.value: An edition-level selected compilation of the Middle English prose Brut through 1333 and independently transmitted continuations, extracts, appendices, and poems selected by Friedrich W. D. Brie. The selected text ends in 1475 and includes substantive prose and verse, including John Page's Siege of Rouen poem within section D; U-M retains the selection as APG1531 while omitting most carrier paratext.

Fifteen ordered edition/XML components occupy printed pages 1-289 and 291-604. Printed page 290 is absent; the encoding follows carrier PDF pages 23-311 and 331-644 and excludes PDF pages 312-330 of Part II paratext.

The record describes Brie's selected edition-level compilation, not one homogeneous anonymous medieval work and not the contents of one manuscript. The selected section K closes in 1475; the U-M header's 1479 wording is retained only as a qualified conflict about the broader continuation tradition.
